Recommendations For Best Practices:
We need to find dataset which is more oriented to health sector infrastructure. This can be hard task, as such datasets are usually  not freely available
More time is needed if trainees are not familiar with basic idea of machine learning
Also, it is expected that trainees are familiar with Python programming language and machine learning, otherwise they will not be able to solve practical assignments by themselves.

Incorporate the latest trends, tools, and threat landscapes to ensure participants are equipped to handle current and emerging cybersecurity threats.
Implement regular testing and certifications to evaluate skill levels and identify areas requiring further development.
Encourage team-based exercises and knowledge-sharing forums to build a community of learners who can exchange ideas and strategies.

To improve learning effectively:
	1.	Use active learning – Encourage discussions, real-life examples, and problem-solving.
	2.	Personalize the process – Adapt to different learning styles and allow flexible pacing.
	3.	Give regular feedback – Provide clear, constructive, and timely feedback.
	4.	Use technology wisely – Integrate interactive tools to boost engagement.
	5.	Support and motivate – Create a safe, encouraging environment and show the real-world value of what’s being learned.

Q37_Value: The material has been overexplained. Jargon used could be explained simply in "human" words and wasn't relevant for the concepts presented because the concepts are quite simple whilst the jargon wasn't. (ex. "Privileges can be given permanently or for a set time", this concept had a whole slide of information that was obvious but the jargon made it very confusing and ruined the pacing. This wouldn't be a problem if most of the slides didn't follow the same logic. Ultimately this led to us not experiencing the practical session to it's fullest extent.) If you're reading this professor, you we're amazing and this shouldn't be a personal critique of your character, but rather a critique of the contents of the slides.

Recommendations For Best Practices:
I ran into a very common workshop challenge — too many participants with varying skill levels and not enough structure for everyone to keep up, especially with something as technical as autoencoders for anomaly detection.
In the future:
- give them a working notebook that already runs end-to-end with minimal code changes needed (e.g., only changing a few parameters).
- add “challenge cells” where more advanced students can modify architecture, play with hyperparameters, or try alternative approaches (e.g., variational autoencoder).
-  have one or two assistants in the room to help those who fall behind
Recommendations For Expanding: Record a walk-through video of the notebook so students can replay at their own pace.
Recommendations For Future Initiatives: Establish a continuous learning program with micro-modules released quarterly to keep pace with emerging threats.
